assertions.title: Dashboard home loading skeleton

steps.action: Sign up with isolated account and complete onboarding to reach dashboard pages
steps.observed: Created isolated user, completed onboarding, and created a dedicated project for downstream route coverage

steps.action: From /dashboard/projects, delay the real GET /api/projects/overview response by 10000ms inside the browser session and navigate to /dashboard
steps.expected: Dashboard home renders skeleton placeholders while the overview request is still in flight
steps.observed: Dashboard showed 61 `.skeleton` elements during the delayed request
`/Loading dashboard/i` check was false; screenshot shows only skeleton placeholders

evidence.network: GET /api/projects/overview -> 200 (captured twice by the app during navigation), each delayed 10000ms in-browser to expose the real loading state; no mocked response body

assertions.notes: Loading was captured by wrapping `window.fetch` in the isolated browser session to pause resolution of matching GET requests while still allowing the real backend response to complete.

assertions.title: Projects list loading skeleton

steps.action: Delay the real GET /api/projects response by 10000ms and navigate to /dashboard/projects
steps.expected: Projects page renders project-card skeletons while data is loading
steps.observed: Projects page rendered 21 `.skeleton` elements across skeleton cards
`/Loading projects/i` check was false while the skeleton grid was visible

evidence.network: GET /api/projects -> 200 (captured twice by the app during navigation), delayed 10000ms in-browser to observe the real loading branch

assertions.notes: Header and New Project action remained visible above the loading skeleton cards.

steps.action: Create a dedicated project and open its detail route /dashboard/projects/8
steps.expected: A real project detail route is available for loading-state validation
steps.observed: Created project 'Loading States Project 20260315' (id 8) and opened its detail page

steps.action: Delay the real GET /api/projects/8 response by 10000ms and navigate to the project detail page
steps.expected: Project detail page renders skeleton placeholders while the request is in flight
steps.observed: Project detail page rendered 40 `.skeleton` elements for title, action area, stats, and list sections
`/Loading project/i` check was false during the loading screenshot

evidence.network: GET /api/projects/8 -> 200 (captured twice by the app during navigation), delayed 10000ms in-browser to expose the real loading UI

steps.action: Delay the real GET /api/projects and GET /api/interviews responses by 10000ms and navigate to /dashboard/interviews
steps.expected: Interviews page renders loading skeleton cards while requests are in flight
The Interviews header and primary action remain visible while skeletons load below
steps.observed: Page title 'Interviews' and the 'New Interview' button were visible above the skeleton cards
`/Loading interviews/i` check was false during the loading screenshot

evidence.network: GET /api/projects -> 200 and GET /api/interviews -> 200 (each observed twice during navigation), all delayed 10000ms in-browser to capture the real loading branch

steps.action: Delay the real GET /api/projects, GET /api/specs, and GET /api/interviews responses by 10000ms and navigate to /dashboard/specs
steps.expected: Specs page renders loading skeleton cards while requests are in flight
The Feature Specs header and actions remain visible while skeletons load below
steps.observed: Page title 'Feature Specs' and the 'New Spec' action were visible above the skeleton cards
`/Loading specs/i` check was false during the loading screenshot

evidence.network: GET /api/projects -> 200, GET /api/specs -> 200, and GET /api/interviews -> 200 (each observed twice during navigation), all delayed 10000ms in-browser to capture the real loading branch

frictions.description: `agent-browser network requests` did not record the app's fetch traffic in this session, so request evidence was captured via an isolated in-browser fetch delay/logger that preserved the real backend responses while exposing the loading branches.
frictions.resolution: Used a temporary `window.fetch` wrapper in the dedicated browser session to delay matching GET requests and record path/status/timing for the report.

summary: Tested 5 loading-state assertions through the real browser UI. All 5 passed: dashboard home, projects list, project detail, interviews, and specs all rendered skeleton placeholders instead of plain loading copy while live requests were delayed in-flight; interviews/specs also kept their headers and primary actions visible.
